verses.1: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed Him.
verses.2: And a man with leprosy came to Him and bowed down before Him, and said, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”
verses.3: Jesus reached out with His hand and touched him, saying, “I am willing; be cleansed.” And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
verses.4: And Jesus said to him, “See that you tell no one; but go, show yourself to the priest and present the offering that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
verses.5: And when Jesus entered Capernaum, a centurion came to Him, begging Him,
verses.6: and saying, “Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, terribly tormented.”
verses.7: Jesus said to him, “I will come and heal him.”
verses.8: But the centurion replied, “Lord, I am not worthy for You to come under my roof, but just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
verses.9: For I also am a man under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this!’ and he does it. ”
verses.10: Now when Jesus heard this, He was amazed and said to those who were following, “Truly I say to you, I have not found such great faith with anyone in Israel.
verses.11: And I say to you that many will come from east and west, and recline at the table with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven;
verses.12: but the sons of the kingdom will be thrown out into the outer darkness; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
verses.13: And Jesus said to the centurion, “Go; it shall be done for you as you have believed.” And the servant was healed at that very moment.
verses.14: When Jesus came into Peter’s home, He saw his mother-in-law lying sick in bed with a fever.
verses.15: And He touched her hand, and the fever left her; and she got up and waited on Him.
verses.16: Now when evening came, they brought to Him many who were demon-possessed; and He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were ill.
verses.17: This happened so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet would be fulfilled: “HE HIMSELF TOOK OUR ILLNESSES AND CARRIED AWAY OUR DISEASES.”
verses.18: Now when Jesus saw a crowd around Him, He gave orders to depart to the other side of the sea.
verses.19: Then a scribe came and said to Him, “Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go.”
verses.20: And Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.”
verses.21: And another of the disciples said to Him, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”
verses.22: But Jesus said to him, “Follow Me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
verses.23: When He got into the boat, His disciples followed Him.
verses.24: And behold, a violent storm developed on the sea, so that the boat was being covered by the waves; but Jesus Himself was asleep.
verses.25: And they came to Him and woke Him, saying, “Save us, Lord; we are perishing!”
verses.26: He said to them, “Why are you afraid, you men of little faith?” Then He got up and rebuked the winds and the sea, and it became perfectly calm.
verses.27: The men were amazed, and said, “What kind of a man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey Him?”
verses.28: And when He came to the other side into the country of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men confronted Him as they were coming out of the tombs. They were so extremely violent that no one could pass by that way.
verses.29: And they cried out, saying, “What business do You have with us, Son of God? Have You come here to torment us before the time?”
verses.30: Now there was a herd of many pigs feeding at a distance from them.
verses.31: And the demons begged Him, saying, “If You are going to cast us out, send us into the herd of pigs.”
verses.32: And He said to them, “Go!” And they came out and went into the pigs; and behold, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned in the waters.
verses.33: And the herdsmen ran away, and went to the city and reported everything, including what had happened to the demon-possessed men.
verses.34: And behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw Him, they pleaded with Him to leave their region.
